title: Severe Heatwave Grips Odisha, Jharsuguda Records 43.3°C - pragativadi.com
full_text:
Odisha reeled under extreme heat on Sunday as half of the state recorded temperatures above 40°C, with Jharsuguda emerging as the hottest city at 43.3°C.
The India Meteorological Department confirmed that 14 cities crossed the 40°C mark. Titilagarh followed Jharsuguda with 43°C, while Sonepur registered 42.3°C. Sambalpur and Bhawanipatna recorded 42°C, and Balangir, Sundargarh, and Rourkela reported 41.5°C. The relentless heatwave has left residents struggling to cope with soaring temperatures.
The IMD forecast indicated that the severe heatwave will continue until April 23. Officials urged people to avoid outdoor activities during peak hours, stay hydrated, and take necessary precautions. The department highlighted that western Odisha districts remain particularly vulnerable to rising temperatures.
The oppressive heat has disrupted normal routines across towns and villages. Schools and workplaces reported reduced attendance as people sought shelter indoors. Health authorities advised citizens to remain cautious, especially children and the elderly, who face higher risks during prolonged exposure.

full_text:
Heatwave continues above 40°c across Odisha
The intense heatwave continues to grip Odisha, with temperatures soaring above 40°C in many parts of the state.
Bhubaneswar: The intense heatwave continues to grip Odisha, with temperatures soaring above 40°C in many parts of the state. Western and interior regions are witnessing severe heat conditions, making daily life increasingly difficult.
According to weather officials, the impact of the heatwave is expected to persist until April 23. Temperatures in interior Odisha may range between 38°C and 40°C, with a further rise of 2 to 3 degrees Celsius likely in the coming days.
Districts including Sundargarh, Jharsuguda, Balangir, Nuapada, and Sambalpur are expected to remain under the influence of the heatwave. Coastal areas, on the other hand, are experiencing high humidity along with rising temperatures, adding to public discomfort.
Even nighttime temperatures in districts like Sundargarh, Balangir, and Sambalpur are likely to remain high, offering little relief. No significant change in temperature is expected over the next 6 to 7 days.
The Meteorological Department has issued advisories urging people to take precautions. Residents are advised to stay hydrated and avoid stepping out between 11 AM and 4 PM unless absolutely necessary to prevent heat-related illnesses.

title: Thunderstorms, heatwave and humidity to impact Odisha over next 5 days - Odisha TV

full_text:
Odisha is set to witness a mix of thunderstorms, rising temperatures, and humid weather conditions over the next five days, according to the latest weather forecast issued by the meteorological department. The outlook indicates fluctuating weather patterns across the state, with intermittent rainfall in several districts and heatwave-like conditions in others.
Rainfall and Thunderstorm Activity
Light to moderate rain or thunderstorms are likely to occur at isolated places across multiple districts, especially in coastal, southern, and tribal regions. Districts such as Puri, Khordha, Ganjam, Gajapati, Rayagada, Koraput, and Malkangiri are expected to experience recurring thunderstorm activity over the forecast period. Northern districts like Mayurbhanj and Keonjhar may also witness similar conditions intermittently.
The weather department has issued a Yellow Warning for thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and gusty winds reaching speeds of 30–40 kmph, particularly during afternoon and evening hours. These conditions could impact normal life, especially in rural and hilly areas, and residents have been advised to remain cautious.
Heatwave and Rising Temperatures
Despite the likelihood of rain in some regions, parts of western Odisha are expected to reel under heatwave conditions.
Districts including Sambalpur, Jharsuguda, Balangir, and Sundargarh are likely to experience intense heat from Day 3 onwards. The heatwave conditions may further expand to districts like Kalahandi, Bargarh, and Nuapada in the following days.
Additionally, warm night conditions are expected to persist in isolated areas such as Bolangir and Sambalpur, making it uncomfortable for residents even after sunset.
Coastal Belt To Experience Humid Weather
Coastal districts, including Balasore, Bhadrak, Jajpur, Kendrapara, Cuttack, Jagatsinghpur, Puri, and Khordha are likely to face hot and humid conditions throughout the forecast period. The combination of high temperature and humidity may lead to discomfort and health concerns, particularly for vulnerable groups.
Day-wise Weather Trend
Day 1–2: Scattered thunderstorms with lightning in northern and southern districts; humid conditions along the coast.
Day 3: Expansion of thunderstorm activity to more districts, along with the onset of heatwave conditions in western Odisha.
Day 4–5: Reduced rainfall activity, mainly confined to southern districts, while heatwave conditions intensify in western parts and humidity persists in coastal areas.
Advisory and Preparedness
Authorities have urged people to stay alert during thunderstorm hours, avoid open fields, and take precautions against lightning strikes. In heatwave-prone areas, residents are advised to limit outdoor activities during peak hours, stay hydrated, and follow health advisories.

Overall, Odisha is expected to experience contrasting weather conditions over the coming days, with thunderstorms offering temporary relief in some regions, even as heat and humidity continue to dominate others.
